#### Lemma 1: `Proj.awayι_app_basicOpen` — port of `IsAffineOpen.fromSpec_app_self`

Mathematical content (prose, NO Lean code):

For a graded ring `𝒜 : ℕ → σ` (in `A`), a homogeneous element
`f ∈ 𝒜 m` of positive degree `m > 0`, the canonical Spec-morphism
`Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm : Spec(HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f) ⟶ Proj 𝒜`
factors through the basic open `D₊(f) ⊂ Proj 𝒜`. Its section-level map at
`U = D₊(f)` (`(Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm).app (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f)`) admits
an explicit formula:

```
(Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm).app (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f)
  = (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫
    (Spec _).presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [opensRange_awayι])).op
```

The proof MIRRORS the proof of `IsAffineOpen.fromSpec_app_self` in
Mathlib (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean:560-564`) and
proceeds in 4 steps:

1. Unfold `awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm = basicOpenIsoSpec.inv ≫ basicOpen.ι`
   via the standard `Proj.awayι_eq_isoSpec_ι_comp` (or its iter-195
   analogist-named equivalent). Apply `Scheme.Hom.comp_app`:
   `(g₁ ≫ g₂).app U = g₂.app U ≫ g₁.app(g₂⁻¹ᵁ U)`.

2. The preimage `basicOpen.ι ⁻¹ᵁ basicOpen 𝒜 f = ⊤` (because
   `basicOpen.ι` is the open embedding of `D₊(f)` into `Proj 𝒜` —
   pulling back `D₊(f)` to itself gives the whole subscheme).
   Hence `(basicOpen.ι).app(basicOpen 𝒜 f) = (Proj 𝒜).presheaf.map
   (eqToHom).op`.

3. `basicOpenIsoSpec.inv.app ⊤ = (basicOpenToSpec.app ⊤)⁻¹` by
   `basicOpenIsoSpec_hom`. Then Mathlib's
   `basicOpenToSpec_app_top` at
   `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.lean:143` gives:
   `(basicOpenToSpec).app ⊤ = ΓSpecIso.hom ≫ awayToSection 𝒜 f ≫
   (basicOpen 𝒜 f).topIso.inv`.

4. Invert the formula in (3) and combine with (2) — the algebraic
   simplification produces exactly the RHS of the lemma statement.

129	**Every declaration block that derives from external reference material requires three citation elements:**

19	The genus-\(0\) arm of the Jacobian-witness construction has a \emph{trivial} witness object
20	(\(J = \Spec k\)); its only remaining mathematical content is a rigidity \emph{statement}:
21	every pointed morphism from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve of genus \(0\)
22	into an abelian variety is the constant morphism at the identity. We prove this
23	\textbf{characteristic-free} --- the protected goal is over an arbitrary field \(k\), so the
24	cheaper differential argument (via $H^0(\mathbb P^1, \Omega) = H^0(\mathbb P^1, \mathcal
25	O(-2)) = 0\(, hence \)df = 0\(, hence \)f$ constant) is unavailable: it breaks on a
26	characteristic-\(p\) Frobenius-descent wall, where a nonconstant \(f\) can have \(df = 0\). The
27	rigidity route below sidesteps that wall.
28	The chain has two parts. The \emph{Rigidity Lemma} (\cref{thm:rigidity_lemma}, Mumford,
29	Form~I; equivalently Milne's Rigidity Theorem~1.1) is an elementary properness/closed-map
30	argument --- it uses neither the theorem of the cube nor any cohomology, is the entry point
31	for formalisation, and is now proven axiom-clean. From the Rigidity Lemma, Milne's \S I.3
32	derives the genus-\(0\) base case \emph{without} the theorem of the cube --- and, in this
33	project's reading, \emph{without} Milne's Theorem~3.2 (rational-map extension), Lemma~3.3,
34	Auslander--Buchsbaum, or differentials (the ``4th route''; see
35	\cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}). The decisive observation is that \(\mathbb P^1\) is
36	\emph{already complete} and \(f \colon \mathbb P^1 \to A\) is \emph{already a total morphism}, so
37	there is no rational defect map to extend over a complete surface and Theorem~3.2 never enters.
38	Instead the base case is closed by the \textbf{\(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling shortcut} (the project's
39	primary route, an Archon-original realisation of Milne Proposition~3.9): one uses the
40	\emph{multiplicative scaling} action $\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to
41	\mathbb P^1\(, \)(x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda x\( (the M\"obius scaling fixing both \)0$ and
42	\(\infty\)), which is a \emph{genuine total} scheme morphism on all of $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb
43	G_m\( (\cref{def:gaTranslationP1}). Normalising \)f(0) = \eta_A\( and composing \)h := \sigma_\times
44	\fatsemi f\(, one applies additivity of \)\mathrm{Hom}(-, A)$ over a product
45	(\cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}, Milne Corollary~1.5) --- whose Lean signature requires
46	only the \emph{first} factor complete --- with \(V = \mathbb P^1\) proper, \(W = \mathbb G_m\), and
47	base points \(0 \in \mathbb P^1\), \(1 \in \mathbb G_m\). Because \(0\) is a \emph{fixed point} of
48	scaling, the \(W\)-axis restriction \(\lambda \mapsto h(0, \lambda) = f(\lambda \cdot 0) = f(0)\) is
49	\emph{constant} at \(\eta_A\), so the decomposition collapses to \(h = p \fatsemi f\), i.e.\
50	\(f(\lambda x) = f(x)\). Specialising \(x = 1\) gives that \(f|_{\mathbb G_m}\) is constant, and since
51	\(\mathbb G_m\) is dense in \(\mathbb P^1\) with \(A\) separated, \(f\) is constant
52	(\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, via the separated-target/dominant-source handle
53	\cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen}). This route uses \emph{only} the proven Corollary~1.5, a
54	scaling fixed point, and density --- it never forms the additive defect map, never invokes
55	\(\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0\), and so sidesteps the ``image of a group homomorphism is a
56	closed subgroup'' input that has no Mathlib support. The classical \(\mathbb G_a\)-additive
57	argument (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial} via the \(\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m\) incompatibility
58	\cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}, the heart of Milne Proposition~3.9) is retained only as the
59	Milne-faithful alternative and is \emph{off} the genus-\(0\) critical path. The corollary that a
60	pointed regular map of abelian
61	varieties is a homomorphism (\cref{lem:av_regular_map_is_hom}, Milne Corollary~1.2) is recorded
62	for completeness and for Route~A but is \emph{not} needed on the genus-\(0\) path; Milne's
63	Theorem~3.2 (\cref{lem:rational_map_to_av_extends}) is likewise demoted to a Route-A-only item
64	(the Albanese universal property), no longer on the genus-\(0\) critical path
65	(\cref{rmk:thm32_codim1_mathlib_gap}). Combining these
66	gives that every morphism \(\mathbb P^1 \to A\) is constant
67	(\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}); composing with the classification of genus-\(0\)
68	curves (\cref{thm:genus_zero_curve_iso_p1}, \(C \cong \mathbb P^1\) over \(\bar k\)) yields the
69	headline \cref{prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}. The theorem of the cube (Milne \S I.5) is
70	\emph{not} used anywhere on this path (see \cref{rmk:cube_not_needed}); the chain invokes
71	neither Serre duality nor the FGA \(\Pic\) representability engine, so the genus-\(0\) arm is
72	fully decoupled from Route~A.
73	\medskip
74	Throughout, \(\bar k\) is an algebraically closed field of arbitrary characteristic. An
75	\emph{abelian variety} over \(\bar k\) is a complete (proper) irreducible group variety; in the
76	project's Lean encoding \(A\) is an object of \(\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)\) carrying
77	\(\mathtt{GrpObj}\), \(\mathtt{IsProper}\), \(\mathtt{Smooth}\), and \(\mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}\).

223	  % any varieties, and \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) a morphism such that for some
224	  % \(y_0 \in Y\), \(f(X \times \{y_0\})\) is a single point \(z_0\) of \(Z\). Then there is a

228	  dense-open construction is the body of that proof).}
229	  Let \(X\) be a complete variety and \(Y\), \(Z\) any varieties over \(\bar k\). Let
230	  \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) be a morphism, fix a point \(x_0 \in X\), and suppose given
231	  \(y_0 \in Y\), \(z_0 \in Z\) with the \emph{collapse hypothesis}
232	  \[ f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\} \qquad
233	     \text{(in Lean: } \mathtt{lift}\,(\mathbf 1_X)\,(\mathtt{toUnit}\,X \fatsemi y_0) \fatsemi f
234	     = \mathtt{toUnit}\,X \fatsemi z_0\text{).} \]
235	  Then there is a non-empty open subset \(U \subseteq X \times Y\) on which \(f\) agrees, as a
236	  scheme morphism, with the collapsed map \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\), where
237	  $\mathtt{retract} := \mathtt{lift}\,(\mathtt{toUnit}\,(X \times Y) \fatsemi x_0)\,
238	  (p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y)\( is the ``\)(x, y) \mapsto (x_0, y)$'' endomorphism. Concretely
239	  \(U = X \times V\) with \(V := Y - G\), \(G = p_2(f^{-1}(Z - U_0))\) for an affine open
240	  neighbourhood \(U_0\) of \(z_0\), and on \(U\) one has \(f(x, y) = f(x_0, y)\).
241	  \medskip
242	  \noindent\textbf{The collapse hypothesis is load-bearing.} The non-emptiness of \(V\) is exactly
243	  the assertion \(y_0 \notin G\), and this holds \emph{because} $f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\}
244	  \subseteq U_0\( keeps the slice over \)y_0\( inside the affine \)U_0\(, away from \)F = Z - U_0$. A
245	  version of this lemma \emph{without} the collapse hypothesis is false: for \(X = Y = Z\) and
246	  \(f = p_1\) the first projection, \(f\) and \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f \colon (x, y) \mapsto x_0\)
247	  agree on no non-empty open (the agreement locus is contained in \(\{x = x_0\}\), which has empty
248	  interior in the irreducible \(X \times Y\)). Therefore the formal target \emph{must} carry the
249	  collapse hypothesis as an explicit antecedent; dropping it (as an earlier decomposition
250	  silently did) makes the deferred goal unsatisfiable.
251	  \medskip
252	  \noindent\textbf{Formalization note: algebraically closed base, and locally-of-finite-type
253	  source.} The prose already takes \(\bar k\) algebraically closed, but the formal statement must
254	  \emph{encode} two properties. First, the cohomology-free proof of the slice-constancy bridge
255	  (below) needs each per-closed-point residue field \(\kappa(y)\) to be \(\bar k\) itself, so that a
256	  proper integral slice has global sections \(\Gamma = \bar k\) and maps to a single \(\bar k\)-point of
257	  the affine \(U_0\); this requires \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]\). Second, the globalisation of the
258	  per-slice agreement (Step~2 of the bridge, \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}) needs the
259	  closed points of the saturated open \(U\) to be \emph{dense} --- i.e.\ \(U\) to be a Jacobson space.
260	  Over the algebraically closed \(\bar k\) this holds precisely because \(X \times Y\) is \emph{locally of
261	  finite type} over \(\bar k\) (\texttt{LocallyOfFiniteType.jacobsonSpace} transports the Jacobson
262	  property of \(\Spec \bar k\), and \texttt{closure\_closedPoints} then gives density); this requires
263	  \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\). Accordingly the formalization carries
264	  \emph{both} \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]\) and
265	  \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\) on this lemma (in addition to
266	  \([\mathtt{Field}\ \bar k]\)), and propagates the same two instances to \cref{thm:rigidity_lemma},
267	  its core helper \texttt{rigidity\_core}, and the bridge sub-lemmas
268	  \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine} and
269	  \cref{lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine}. Both cost nothing downstream: the
270	  consumers \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant} and \cref{prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} (Lean
271	  \texttt{rigidity\_genus0\_curve\_to\_grpScheme}) \emph{already} assume
272	  \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]\) --- the base-field variable is literally named \(\mathtt{kbar}\) ---
273	  and curves and abelian varieties are of finite type over \(\bar k\), so the
274	  locally-of-finite-type instance is automatic there.
275	\end{lemma}

291	  collapse hypothesis \(f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\} \subseteq U_0\) gives

295	  into the affine \(U_0\), hence to a single point, necessarily \(f(x_0, y)\). So for all \(x \in X\),

297	  \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\) agree on the non-empty open \(U := X \times V\). (In the Lean

1656	      \(\Delta_{\overline{\mathbb P^1}}\)}. By Mathlib's
1657	      \(\mathtt{IsClosedImmersion.lift\_iff\_range\_subset}\) (the universal property
1658	      of a closed immersion: a morphism factors through a closed immersion iff its
1659	      set-theoretic image is contained in the image of the immersion), the
1660	      factorization is equivalent to the set-level containment of the cocycle's image
1661	      in the image of \(\Delta\), which on points is exactly the chart equality on
1662	      the \emph{closed points} of the intersection. The closed-point check is
1663	      discrete (each closed point of the intersection lies over a single \(\bar k\)-
1664	      point of \(D_+(X_0 \cdot X_1) \times \mathbb G_m\) and the two chart maps both
1665	      evaluate to the same \(\bar k\)-point of \(\overline{\mathbb P^1}\)
1666	      corresponding to that \((x, \lambda)\)), and bypasses the simp coverage gap
1667	      that obstructs (III.a)/(III.b) entirely.
1668	      \emph{Concrete prover recipe.} The path closes in four steps:
1669	      \begin{enumerate}
1670	        \item Fix the diagonal
1671	          \(\Delta_{\overline{\mathbb P^1}} \colon \overline{\mathbb P^1}
1672	            \longrightarrow \overline{\mathbb P^1} \times_{\bar k}
1673	            \overline{\mathbb P^1}\)
1674	          via Mathlib's \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Limits.prod.lift}\,(\mathbf
1675	          1_{\overline{\mathbb P^1}})\,(\mathbf 1_{\overline{\mathbb P^1}})\), and
1676	          obtain the closed-immersion witness from
1677	          \(\mathtt{IsSeparated.diagonal\_isClosedImmersion}\) applied to the
1678	          separated structure morphism
1679	          \(\overline{\mathbb P^1}.\mathrm{hom} \colon \overline{\mathbb P^1} \to
1680	          \Spec \bar k\).
1681	        \item Build the pair-morphism
1682	          \((\mathrm{chart}_0, \mathrm{chart}_1) \colon
1683	            \mathrm{pullback}\,((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover})_0.f)\,
1684	              ((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover})_1.f)
1685	            \longrightarrow
1686	            \overline{\mathbb P^1} \times_{\bar k} \overline{\mathbb P^1}\)
1687	          via \(\mathtt{prod.lift}\) on the two named chart restrictions above.
1688	        \item Show this pair factors through \(\Delta_{\overline{\mathbb P^1}}\)
1689	          via the universal property of the intersection's pullback structure
1690	          combined with \(\mathtt{IsClosedImmersion.lift}\) (kernel-inequality
1691	          form; the \(\mathtt{\_iff\_range\_subset}\) variant is NOT in Mathlib
1692	          at \texttt{b80f227} per iter-188 verification, so this step requires
1693	          either upstream contribution or a project-side bridge plus
1694	          \(\mathtt{IsReduced}\) on the intersection): produce the section
1695	          \(\mathrm{(intersection)} \to \overline{\mathbb P^1}\) whose post-
1696	          composition with \(\Delta\) equals \((\mathrm{chart}_0,
1697	          \mathrm{chart}_1)\).
1698	        \item The factorization reduces, by composing with the two projections
1699	          \(\mathtt{prod.fst}\) and \(\mathtt{prod.snd}\) out of the codomain
1700	          \(\overline{\mathbb P^1} \times_{\bar k} \overline{\mathbb P^1}\), to
1701	          two scheme-equalities on the components --- each of which is a direct
1702	          chart-bridge equality on \((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover})_i\)'s structure
1703	          (i.e.\ the \emph{single-chart} content, not the full cocycle), discharged
1704	          using the same chart-ring iso \cref{def:proj_chart_ring_iso} and
1705	          \(\mathtt{pullbackSpecIso}\) bridge that \cref{def:gmscaling_chart} uses.
1706	          The chart-\(1\) instance of the per-component equality threads
1707	          through the named \(\mathrm{Proj.appIso}\) evaluation
1708	          \cref{lem:iotaGm_chart1_appIso_eval}, which packages the basic-open
1709	          ring-map identification
1710	          \(\mathtt{isLocElem} \mapsto [X_0/X_1] \mapsto 1\) at the global-sections
1711	          level so the prover can target it directly without re-deriving the
1712	          \((\mathrm{Proj.appIso}).\mathrm{inv}\) image of \(\mathtt{isLocElem}\)
1713	          inline (the iter-188 through iter-191 budget-wall step). The chart-\(0\)
1714	          instance is the symmetric statement with roles of \(X_0, X_1\)
1715	          interchanged.
1716	      \end{enumerate}
1717	      \emph{Substrate hooks.} The categorical substrate is present at the
1718	      pinned Mathlib commit \texttt{b80f227}:
1719	      \(\mathtt{IsSeparated.diagonal\_isClosedImmersion}\),
1720	      \(\mathtt{IsClosedImmersion.lift}\) (kernel-inequality form),
1721	      \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Limits.prod.lift}\),
1722	      \(\mathtt{pullback.lift}\), together with the standard
1723	      \(\mathtt{IsSeparated}\) instance for \(\Proj\) of a graded ring (Stacks
1724	      \texttt{01KU} / Hartshorne~II.4.6 diagonal-is-closed characterisation).
1725	      % NOTE (iter-188 review): the \texttt{\_iff\_range\_subset} variant of
1726	      % \texttt{IsClosedImmersion.lift} cited in the (III.c) recipe is NOT present
1727	      % at b80f227 (verified iter-188 via lean\_leansearch on
1728	      % \enquote{morphism factors through closed immersion iff range subset}
1729	      % and \enquote{IsClosedImmersion lift range subset}; both return only the
1730	      % kernel-inequality form). Closure requires either a Mathlib upstream
1731	      % contribution of \texttt{IsClosedImmersion.lift\_iff\_range\_subset} or a
1732	      % project-side build that also threads
1733	      % \texttt{Algebra.TensorProduct.isDomain\_of\_isAlgClosed\_left}-style
1734	      % reducedness of the intersection. See
1735	      % \texttt{.archon/task\_results/AlgebraicJacobian\_Genus0BaseObjects\_GmScaling.lean.md}
1736	      % \S\enquote{Mathlib lemmas confirmed NOT SHIPPED}.
1737	      \emph{Cost estimate (post iter-188 falsification).} \(\sim 150\)--\(200\)
1738	      LOC over 3--5 iters (Option B: project-side substrate including
1739	      \(\mathtt{lift\_iff\_range\_subset}\) plus \(\mathtt{IsReduced}\) on the
1740	      intersection); Option A (Mathlib upstream) is unbounded in time.
1741	  \end{description}
1742	  \medskip
1743	  \noindent\textbf{(IV) Substantive ring-level residual.} All three pickup paths reduce,
1744	  after their structural setup, to the same ring-level identity. On the basic-open
1745	  intersection \(D_+(X_0 \cdot X_1) \subseteq \overline{\mathbb P^1}\) both chart
1746	  coordinates are units (the chart-\(0\) coordinate \(t = X_1/X_0\) and the chart-\(1\)
1747	  coordinate \(u = X_0/X_1\) are reciprocals: \(t \cdot u = 1\)), and the agreement
1748	  reduces to the ring-level identity
1749	  \[ \lambda \cdot u \ =\ \frac{1}{t} \cdot \lambda \quad
1750	     \text{in } \mathrm{Localization.Away}\,t \otimes_{\bar k} \mathtt{GmRing}\,\bar k, \]
1751	  i.e.\ to the unit-of-the-localisation identity \(t \cdot u = 1\)
1752	  (\(\mathtt{IsLocalization.Away.mul\_invSelf}\)) together with commutativity of the
1753	  tensor product. The proof routes through the chart-ring iso
1754	  \cref{def:proj_chart_ring_iso} and the same \(\mathtt{pullbackSpecIso}\) bridge that
1755	  \cref{def:gmscaling_chart} uses; consequently \cref{lem:gmscaling_chart_agreement}
1756	  remains gated on \cref{def:gmscaling_chart} acquiring its concrete body \emph{and} on
1757	  one of the (III.a)--(III.c) structural setups landing. See
1758	  \(\mathtt{analogies/gmscaling{\text-}projection{\text-}idiom.md}\) (iter-184) for the
1759	  full Recipe~1/2/3 decomposition and empirical simp-probe verification.

12	## Structural problem (abstracted)
13	
14	Given a canonical open immersion `f : Spec(R) ⟶ X` whose range is a fixed
15	open `U ⊂ X`, and given the induced section-level isomorphism
16	`α := f.appIso ⊤ : Γ(X, U) ≅ Γ(Spec R, ⊤)`, evaluate `α.inv : Γ(Spec R, ⊤) ⟶ Γ(X, U)`
17	on a specific element `x : R = Γ(Spec R, ⊤)` (here `R = Away 𝒜 f`, `X = Proj 𝒜`,
18	`U = D₊(f)`, `x = isLocElem`).
19	
20	Equivalent abstracted shape: in any concrete category with a "global sections"
21	functor `Γ`, with a canonical iso `α : F(U) ≅ G(V)` between section objects on
22	two distinguished opens, compute the value of `α.inv` on a generator. Direct
23	chasing of the iso definition through the underlying construction fails when
24	the construction is itself a composition of named opaque pieces.
25

  = \mathrm{Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec}\,\mathcal A\,f\,(\mathtt{f\_deg})\,(\mathtt{hm}).\mathrm{inv}
  \,\fatsemi\, (\mathrm{Proj.basicOpen}\,\mathcal A\,f).\iota\) at
  \(\mathtt{Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.lean:189}\)
  (or via the project's \(\mathtt{Proj.awayι\_eq\_isoSpec\_ι\_comp}\)
  analogue), the section-level map at \(\mathrm{Proj.basicOpen}\,\mathcal A\,f\)
  factors. Apply \(\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.comp\_app}\)
  \((g_1 \fatsemi g_2).\mathrm{app}\,U = g_2.\mathrm{app}\,U
   \,\fatsemi\, g_1.\mathrm{app}(g_2^{-1}\,U)\)
  to split the section map into the
  \((\mathrm{Proj.basicOpen}\,\mathcal A\,f).\iota.\mathrm{app}\) factor at \(U\)
  followed by the \(\mathrm{Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec}.\mathrm{inv}.\mathrm{app}\) factor
  at the preimage \((\mathrm{Proj.basicOpen}\,\mathcal A\,f).\iota^{-1}\,U\).
  \medskip
  \noindent\textbf{Step 2 (collapse the open-embedding preimage).} The
  preimage of the basic open under its own open-embedding inclusion is the
  whole subscheme:
  \((\mathrm{Proj.basicOpen}\,\mathcal A\,f).\iota^{-1}\,
  (\mathrm{Proj.basicOpen}\,\mathcal A\,f) = \top\). Hence
  \((\mathrm{Proj.basicOpen}\,\mathcal A\,f).\iota.\mathrm{app}\,
  (\mathrm{Proj.basicOpen}\,\mathcal A\,f) = (\Proj\,\mathcal A).\mathrm{presheaf.map}
  \,(\mathrm{eqToHom}\,(\ldots)).\mathrm{op}\) is a pure \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\)
  presheaf-map adjustment by the canonical open-embedding-of-an-open-subscheme
  identity (\(\mathtt{Opens.\iota\_app}\) in Mathlib).
  \medskip
  \noindent\textbf{Step 3 (the substrate identity at \(\top\)).} The
  \(\mathrm{Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec}.\mathrm{inv}\) factor evaluates at \(\top\) by
  \(\mathrm{Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec}.\mathrm{inv}.\mathrm{app}\,\top
  = (\mathrm{Proj.basicOpenToSpec}.\mathrm{app}\,\top)^{-1}\) (from
  \(\mathtt{Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec\_hom}\) which inverts to give the inverse
  on sections). Mathlib's
  \(\mathtt{Proj.basicOpenToSpec\_app\_top}\) at
  \(\mathtt{Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.lean:143}\)
  then supplies the explicit formula
  \[ (\mathrm{Proj.basicOpenToSpec}\,\mathcal A\,f\,(\mathtt{f\_deg})\,(\mathtt{hm}))
       .\mathrm{app}\,\top
     \ =\
     (\mathrm{Scheme}.\Gamma\mathrm{SpecIso}\,\_).\mathrm{hom}
       \,\fatsemi\,
     \mathrm{awayToSection}\,\mathcal A\,f
       \,\fatsemi\,
     (\mathrm{Proj.basicOpen}\,\mathcal A\,f).\mathrm{topIso.inv}, \]
  where \(\mathrm{awayToSection}\,\mathcal A\,f \colon \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,f
  \to \Gamma(\Proj\,\mathcal A,\,\mathrm{basicOpen}\,\mathcal A\,f)\) is the
  canonical (forward) ring map that
  \(\mathrm{Proj.basicOpenIsoAway}.\mathrm{hom}\) is implemented as. Inverting
  this factor identifies it with
  \((\mathrm{Proj.basicOpen}\,\mathcal A\,f).\mathrm{topIso.hom}
  \,\fatsemi\, \mathrm{Proj.basicOpenIsoAway}.\mathrm{inv}
  \,\fatsemi\, (\mathrm{Scheme}.\Gamma\mathrm{SpecIso}\,\_).\mathrm{inv}\).
  \medskip
  \noindent\textbf{Step 4 (combine).} Composing the result of Step~2 (the
  \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\) factor) with the result of Step~3 (the inverted
  \(\mathrm{Proj.basicOpenToSpec}.\mathrm{app}\,\top\) factor) and rearranging
  the canonical \(\mathrm{topIso}\) adjustments yields exactly the RHS of the
  lemma statement. The algebraic simplification is mechanical
  (\(\mathtt{Iso.inv\_hom\_id\_assoc}\) cancels the \(\mathrm{topIso}\) round-trip;
  the surviving \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\) presheaf-map is the one against
  \(\mathtt{opensRange\_awayι}\) listed in the conclusion). This mirrors the
  three-line Mathlib proof of \(\mathtt{IsAffineOpen.fromSpec\_app\_self}\)
  step-for-step, replacing
  \(\mathtt{IsAffineOpen.isoSpec}\) with \(\mathrm{Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec}\),

  \(\mathrm{Proj}\)-side companion
  \(\mathtt{Proj.basicOpenToSpec\_app\_top}\). LOC estimate: \(\sim 10\)--\(15\).
\end{proof}
\begin{lemma}

[Point-value evaluation of \(((\mathrm{Proj.awayι}).\mathrm{appIso}\,\top).\mathrm{inv}\)
on the canonical generator \(\mathtt{isLocElem}\)]

  \(\Spec\)-identity-ring-map under the
